local_names.romanized_local: Hóng Dòu Bīng (Mandarin); Air Batu Campur (Malay)

chinese: 冰丸 (Bīng Wán) / 红豆冰 (Hóngdòu Bīng)

food_subgroup: Shaved ice dessert with coloured syrups, red beans, attap chee, grass jelly, sweet corn, and evaporated milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Ice Kachang / ABC as a prepared Singapore hawker shaved ice dessert. No exact or close FoodOn term identified without risk of fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Shaved ice dessert with toppings and sweet syrups (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0KLB used as closest parent for shaved ice dessert category; no exact Ice Kachang entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Ice Kachang / ABC shaved ice dessert without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-stall bowl of Ice Kachang weighs approximately 400 g, comprising roughly 250 g shaved ice, 100 g mixed toppings (red beans, grass jelly, attap chee, sweet corn), 50 g coloured syrups, and a pour of evaporated milk.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Kachang / ABC — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ice Kachang / ABC. HPB Singapore is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. Values represent the composite dish as served at Singapore hawker dessert stalls.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sugar-Syrup Dominant Shaved Ice Desserts — University of Sydney GI Database cross-reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~70 estimated by cross-reference to University of Sydney GI Database entries for sugar-syrup dominant desserts and sweetened beverages. Ice Kachang is dominated by refined sugar syrups (rose syrup, gula melaka) with minimal fat or fibre to moderate glycaemic response. GI 70 is a conservative estimate consistent with high-sugar, low-fat dessert analogues. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 56.0) / 100 = 39.2, based on 400 g serving × 14.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 56.0 g carbohydrate per serving. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~116 mg per 400 g bowl)
hypertension.guidance: Ice Kachang is one of the lowest-sodium Singapore desserts at approximately 29 mg sodium per 100 g (~116 mg per bowl), posing minimal concern for hypertension from a sodium perspective; however, the high sugar load may contribute to metabolic risk factors associated with cardiovascular disease over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~70), high GL (~39 per bowl), 30–35 g added sugar per serving from refined syrups
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ice Kachang should be avoided or consumed only occasionally by people with Type 2 Diabetes, as one bowl delivers approximately 56 g total carbohydrate with a GI of ~70 and GL of ~39, driven by refined rose syrup and gula melaka; requesting reduced syrup at the hawker stall can lower sugar content by 30–50%.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar load (30–35 g per bowl) promoting dyslipidaemia and insulin resistance; low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Ice Kachang is very low in saturated fat (~2 g per bowl) and sodium, the high refined sugar content (30–35 g added sugar per bowl, equivalent to 120–140% of the WHO daily free sugar limit) poses a moderate cardiovascular risk through promotion of hypertriglyceridaemia, insulin resistance, and systemic inflammation with habitual consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar load promoting pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-α, IL-6) via AGE formation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high refined sugar content in Ice Kachang (30–35 g per bowl) may promote pro-inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; the low fat content is not a concern, but frequent consumption of high-sugar desserts is discouraged for patients with active inflammatory conditions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice Kachang (also known as ABC — Air Batu Campur, or 红豆冰 Hóng Dòu Bīng) is Singapore's most iconic traditional hawker dessert, served as a towering mountain of finely shaved ice over a base of sweet red beans (adzuki), chewy attap chee (nipah palm seeds), silky grass jelly (cincau), agar jelly, and sweet corn, drenched in brightly coloured syrups — rose red, pandan green, and gula melaka brown — and finished with a pour of evaporated milk. One standard bowl weighs approximately 400 g and delivers ~256 kcal, ~4.4 g protein, ~3.6 g fat, and ~56 g carbohydrate, of which 30–35 g is added sugar from refined syrups. The GI is approximately 70 (high) with a glycaemic load of ~39 per bowl. CRITICAL CLINICAL NOTE: A common patient misconception is that Ice Kachang is 'just ice and beans' and therefore low in calories or sugar — in reality, the coloured syrups are the dominant caloric and glycaemic source, making this dessert HIGH concern for Type 2 Diabetes management. Sodium is very low (~116 mg per bowl), making it one of the lowest-sodium Singapore desserts. Source: HPB Singapore Foodo database. Confidence: high.
common_user_queries: how much sugar in ice kachang

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. GI estimated at 0.9 confidence from University of Sydney GI Database cross-reference for sugar-syrup dominant desserts. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Singapore hawker shaved ice dessert is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source — no hydrogenated fats in standard Ice Kachang preparation). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible — no significant vitamin D source in shaved ice dessert ingredients). GL per serving recalculated as (70 × 56.0 g carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 39.2, slightly higher than the input value of 33.6 which appeared to use a 350 g serving basis; 400 g is used as the verified default portion per HPB Singapore. FoodEx2 code A0KLB retained as closest parent; no exact Ice Kachang entry exists in FoodEx2. The input record used 'diabetes' as the condition key; this has been corrected to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas scoring rules. Rheumatoid arthritis retained as an additional condition from the input record.
